preamble: The shift from the voiceless alveolar stop [t] to the alveolar fricative [s] is known as assibilation. It is frequently triggered by a following high-front vowel [i] or a palatal glide [j], where the stop gains friction and eventually loses its closure.

examples.note: Historical assibilation of t before i.
Assibilation of the dental stop before i + vowel.
